summary: Full Vigilance module stabilization pass (backend + frontend). Backend regression suite test_vigilance_flow.py = 30/30 PASS (access control, template + filters, upload, isolation/merge, CRUD ownership, validation, HH:MM and HH:MM:SS durations, export, attendance-integration, base grid). Frontend admin /vigilance verified fully working: base grid (25+ rows for default Today), sticky-right Actions column (position:sticky;right:0), sticky 2-row header (--vig-h1 now resolves to 44px on admin view — iter49 latent 0px bug appears fixed), Add Entry dialog with all expected fields, all filter testids present, top+body scroll sync wiring confirmed. Non-vigilance user (user/pass123) -> /employee/vigilance correctly renders [data-testid=vigilance-no-access] and exposes no data. CRITICAL BLOCKER discovered: vigilance-designation employees (madhan.s, dinesh.t) cannot reach /employee/vigilance because the EmployeeRoute guard in /app/frontend/src/App.js line 88-101 unconditionally redirects ANY employee with needsOnboarding()===true to /employee/onboarding. madhan.s currently has onboarding_status='under_review' (13% progress), so navigating to /employee/vigilance redirects to /employee/onboarding before OperationalVigilance can mount. This makes the entire vigilance-user side unreachable in the current preview state, even though backend GET /api/vigilance/access returns has_access=true/is_vigilance=true and pytest exercises the API directly. DB cleaned of pytest-fixture residue: 2 stray submissions (one from each of vig1 and vig2 on 05-Jun-2026) were left behind by test_vigilance_flow.py::upload_data fixture (no teardown). Deleted both via admin DELETE; final residual count = 0.

minor.issue: Module-scoped fixture inserts 2 vigilance entries (vig1+vig2 uploads) but never cleans them up. Each pytest run leaves stray vigilance submissions on 05-Jun-2026. User explicitly requested 0 stray entries. Add a yield+teardown that DELETEs both submissions.

ui_bugs.issue: Hard-redirects vigilance-designation employees with non-approved onboarding to /employee/onboarding, making /employee/vigilance unreachable. madhan.s and dinesh.t both currently have onboarding_status='under_review' (13%) and therefore CANNOT use the Vigilance Report module from the UI even though backend has_access=true.

integration_issues.issue: Route never mounts OperationalVigilance for vigilance employees because EmployeeRoute pre-empts with onboarding redirect; UI flow effectively dead until either (a) onboarding is approved for vigilance employees, or (b) the guard carves out an exception for user.designation==='Vigilance' (or any has_access principal) on the /employee/vigilance path.

issues: [Carry-over from iter49] Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}' on every Vigilance dialog open. Add a DialogDescription to EntryDialog for a11y.

[Carry-over from iter48/49] React 'Encountered two children with the same key' warning still printed on every render (sub-header Fragment keys). Use composite keys like `${label}-${sub}` instead of bare `sub`.

action_items: [CRITICAL] Patch EmployeeRoute in /app/frontend/src/App.js (~line 88-101) so that vigilance-designation employees (or any principal whose /api/vigilance/access returns has_access=true) can still reach /employee/vigilance even when onboarding_status!='approved'. Suggested minimal fix: in EmployeeRoute, when location.pathname === '/employee/vigilance' AND user.designation === 'Vigilance' (case-insensitive), skip the onboarding redirect. Alternatively, mark vigilance designation employees as onboarding-bypassed at the user model level.
[CRITICAL] Either approve onboarding for madhan.s and dinesh.t in the preview DB, or implement the per-route bypass above — without one of these the entire vigilance-user UI flow cannot be exercised.
Add teardown to upload_data fixture in test_vigilance_flow.py so each pytest run leaves the vigilance collection clean (the agent had to manually DELETE 2 stray submissions after this run).
Add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to EntryDialog in OperationalVigilance.js to silence the recurring a11y console warning.
Stabilise sub-header Fragment keys in OperationalVigilance.js (use composite keys like `${parentLabel}-${sub}`) to clear the React duplicate-key warning.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js EmployeeRoute uses a single needsOnboarding() gate for all employee routes — too coarse. Pages that depend on designation-based access (Vigilance) need either a per-route allowlist or an onboarding-exempt designation list.

test_vigilance_flow.py::upload_data is a module-scope fixture that mutates real DB state and never cleans up. In a shared preview environment this leaves vigilance data behind that the real team will see. Convert to a yield+teardown pattern that captures the submission ids returned by /api/vigilance/upload (or queries them by uploaded_by + date range) and DELETEs them at module teardown.
OperationalVigilance.js (~1000+ lines, single file) — consider splitting EntryDialog, table+header logic, and filters into separate files to stay under the 700-line guideline.

Onboarding gating: madhan.s and dinesh.t live as 'employees with under_review onboarding' but their designation is Vigilance. If they are meant to operate the Vigilance Report module, the seed/admin path that promotes them to designation=Vigilance should ALSO flip onboarding_completed=true (or onboarding_status='approved') to avoid the UI dead-end.

success_rate.frontend: Admin view 100%, Non-vig blocked correctly. Vigilance-user view 0% reachable due to onboarding-gate blocker.

test_credentials: admin/HrAdmin786$ (verified), madhan.s/Vigil@123 (login OK, redirected to onboarding), dinesh.t/Vigil@123 (not directly retested in this iteration), user/pass123 (verified blocked).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Deleted 2 stray vigilance submissions left behind by pytest upload_data fixture (ids a3d24b87-700d-4cd8-aa14-7a432909c66b and 0747f1c5-fc54-44e0-ad34-3dbdfbfbc431, both on 05-Jun-2026 against employee 'user'). Final residual count = 0 verified via GET /api/vigilance/entries?from_date=01-Jun-2026&to_date=10-Jun-2026.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid (30/30). The ONLY thing blocking full e2e UI validation is the EmployeeRoute onboarding redirect for vigilance-designation users. After E1 patches App.js EmployeeRoute (or marks madhan/dinesh onboarding-approved), re-run a Playwright pass against /employee/vigilance with madhan.s to validate: (a) vig-own-table renders ~50-row base grid; (b) Add entry via vig-edit-btn -> vig-entry-dialog -> save 08:15:30/01:00 renders exactly; (c) vig-delete-btn appears only after entry exists; (d) vig-view-btn opens read-only dialog; (e) sort, top-scroll sync, pagination work; (f) isolation: load as dinesh.t and confirm madhan's entry is invisible. Remember to delete any test entries created. Also after patching the fixture, re-run pytest and confirm DB stays clean.
rca of the issue: EmployeeRoute (frontend/src/App.js:88-101) calls useAuth().needsOnboarding() and unconditionally redirects to /employee/onboarding when true. AuthContext's needsOnboarding() likely returns true whenever user.onboarding_status !== 'approved' || user.onboarding_completed !== true. madhan.s/dinesh.t are seeded with designation='Vigilance' but their onboarding_status remains 'under_review' (Madhan S only has Aadhaar uploaded; PAN, Education, etc. NOT UPLOADED — progress 13%). Therefore any attempt to navigate to /employee/vigilance triggers the redirect before OperationalVigilance can mount. Mitigation: add a per-pathname exception in EmployeeRoute -- e.g. `if (location.pathname.startsWith('/employee/vigilance') && (user.designation || '').toLowerCase() === 'vigilance') return <ProtectedRoute>...<EmployeeLayout>{children}</EmployeeLayout>...</ProtectedRoute>;` BEFORE the onboarding redirect. Reproduction: login madhan.s/Vigil@123, then GET /employee/vigilance — url stays at /employee/onboarding.
